title: IDEX Corporation (IEX) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of IDEX Corporation (IEX) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 7.0% growth rate is assumed, reflecting IDEX's consistent ability to expand margins and execute value-accretive bolt-on acquisitions in highly specialized, mission-critical industrial niches.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 9.0% discount rate reflects the underlying cyclicality of broader industrial markets, balancing IDEX's strong balance sheet against macroeconomic risks.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 3.0% terminal growth rate aligns with historical GDP growth, acknowledging IDEX's durable moat in niche manufacturing segments while reflecting mature industrial dynamics.

thesisParagraphs: IDEX operates in highly fragmented, specialized industrial niches where the cost of product failure far outweighs the cost of the component itself. This dynamic grants IDEX immense pricing power and creates significant switching costs. While the company's valuation currently implies a slight premium, its structural advantages remain intact.

The decentralized operating model allows IDEX to efficiently allocate capital, primarily through continuous bolt-on acquisitions that expand their total addressable market and drive margin expansion. However, at current valuation levels, the market appears to have fully priced in these advantages, leaving a negligible margin of safety for new investors.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via Yahoo Finance (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why did Gemini pick a 7.0% growth rate for IDEX?
faq.answer: Gemini projects a 7.0% free cash flow growth rate based on IDEX's historical ability to compound earnings through a combination of low-single-digit organic growth and consistent, highly accretive acquisitions in niche markets.

faq.question: What discount rate was used for IDEX's DCF?
faq.answer: A 9.0% discount rate was selected. This accounts for the cyclical nature of industrial demand while giving credit to IDEX's strong balance sheet and robust pricing power within its specialized segments.

faq.question: Is IDEX a buy at current prices?
faq.answer: This analysis currently indicates the stock is slightly overvalued, lacking a sufficient margin of safety. This is a demonstration of AI reasoning based on rigid inputs and is not financial advice.
